compiled_state.author: Iso Tester
compiled_state.description:
Each plan-review workflow op independently re-assembles the entire ticket graph from the store, so one gate run does ~4×(1 + 1 + K children) `show_ticket`/`list_tickets` reads. On an epic with many children this is the latency the observability commit (`08cd4c325`) was added to explain — "a slow epic review looks like a hang" — a client-facing reliability/perceived-performance issue.

## Why
The B-RETIRE migration decomposed the review into pure `uses` ops but gave them no shared context, so each re-reads + re-derives the graph (`orchestrator.assemble_context` is itself an N+1 read: `show_ticket` + `list_tickets(parent=)` + one `show_ticket` per child).

## What / files
- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/workflow_ops.py:66,147,212,235` — the precheck/assemble/verify_inputs/coach_inputs ops each call `orchestrator.assemble_context(...)`.
- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/production_batch_runner.py:85` and `__init__.py:108` — assemble again.
- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py:77-110` — `assemble_context` (the N+1 read).
Approach: assemble `PlanContext` ONCE in `plan_review_precheck`, serialize its `state`/`children` into the precheck step outputs, and have downstream ops reconstruct `PlanContext` from those outputs (as `plan_review_coach` already does at `workflow_ops.py:342`). Alternatively memoize on `(run_id, ticket_id)`.

## Scope
In: eliminate the redundant re-reads within one gate run; preserve verdict bytes exactly. Out: changing the verdict/finding shape or the workflow YAML's op sequence.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A single plan-review gate run assembles the ticket graph at most once (assert via the observability step counts / a read-counter spy).
- [ ] Verdict output is byte-identical to pre-change for a fixed fixture (characterization test).
- [ ] Measured store reads for an epic-with-K-children review drop from ~4×(2+K) to ~(2+K).
- [ ] Existing plan-review tests pass.
compiled_state.title: Eliminate redundant ticket-graph re-assembly in plan-review gate
